cuisine: Packaged dairy — Japanese brand sold in Singapore
food_subgroup: Full-cream UHT (ultra-high temperature) processed cow's milk. 1 serving = 200ml carton. UHT processing extends shelf life without refrigeration until opened.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 200ml Meiji Full Cream Milk UHT carton, equivalent to approximately 206g, is the typical single-serve portion consumed as a beverage or used in cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Meiji Full Cream Milk UHT — Meiji Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel; cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy Food #01077 (Whole milk, 3.25% milkfat)
nutrient_source.organization: Meiji Co., Ltd. Singapore product NIP / USDA Agricultural Research Service SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values from Meiji Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) per 100ml, converted to per 100g using density of whole milk (~1.03 g/ml). Micronutrients cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy #01077 for completeness. All core macros are from the verified product label.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Full-Cream Cow's Milk — University of Sydney GI Database (whole milk, full-fat)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 27 (low) sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for full-cream cow's milk. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (27 × 9.476g) / 100 ≈ 2.6. Carbohydrate per serving = 4.6g/100g × 206g = 9.476g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (44mg/100g; 91mg per 200ml serving) with beneficial calcium and potassium
hypertension.guidance: Meiji Full Cream Milk UHT is suitable for hypertensive patients: one 200ml serving provides only 91mg sodium alongside 239mg calcium and 309mg potassium, supporting DASH diet targets and blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (GI 27) and very low glycemic load per serving (GL ~2.6)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Full-cream milk has a very low GI of 27 and a GL of approximately 2.6 per 200ml serving, making it safe for Type 2 Diabetes patients; the disproportionately high insulin index of dairy is clinically beneficial by reducing post-meal glucose excursions without causing hypoglycaemia.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (2.1g/100g; 4.3g per 200ml) from dairy fat matrix with neutral-to-beneficial cardiovascular evidence
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Current evidence from prospective cohort studies and meta-analyses does not support restricting full-cream dairy for cardiovascular disease prevention; the dairy fat matrix and accompanying calcium, potassium, and phosphorus are associated with neutral or modestly beneficial cardiovascular outcomes at one 200ml serving per day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Modest saturated fat with anti-inflammatory micronutrients (calcium, vitamin D, vitamin B12)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Full-cream milk at 200ml per serving provides anti-inflammatory micronutrients relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management including 239mg calcium (bone protection), 0.2mcg vitamin D (immune modulation), and 0.82mcg vitamin B12; RCT evidence does not support dairy avoidance for RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Meiji Full Cream Milk UHT is a premium ultra-high temperature (UHT) processed whole cow's milk produced by Meiji Co., Ltd., a major Japanese food company founded in 1916. It is sold in Singapore in 200ml individual cartons and is one of the most popular premium milk brands in the country, particularly among Chinese-Singaporean households. UHT processing heats milk to approximately 135–150°C for 2–5 seconds, eliminating pathogens and extending shelf life without refrigeration until opened, while preserving the nutritional profile of whole milk. The milk has a clean, mildly sweet, creamy flavour with a smooth texture characteristic of full-fat dairy. Per 100g, it provides 63 kcal, 3.3g protein, 3.5g fat (2.1g saturated), 4.6g carbohydrate (all as lactose), 116mg calcium, 150mg potassium, and 44mg sodium. A standard 200ml serving (206g) delivers approximately 130 kcal, 6.8g protein, 7.2g fat, 9.5g carbohydrate, and 239mg calcium (approximately 24% of the Singapore RDI for calcium). Clinically, full-cream milk is well-suited for patients with Type 2 Diabetes (GI 27, GL ~2.6 per serving), hypertension (low sodium, high calcium and potassium), and cardiovascular disease (neutral-to-beneficial dairy fat matrix per current meta-analytic evidence). The dairy insulin index is higher than its GI, which is beneficial for glycaemic control. Individual lactose intolerance may limit consumption; Meiji also produces a lactose-free variant. Meiji milk is widely available at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and Sheng Siong supermarkets and is commonly consumed as a breakfast beverage, bedtime drink, school canteen staple, and cooking or baking ingredient.
common_user_queries: is full cream milk good for diabetics

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the Meiji Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (verified product label), cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy #01077 for micronutrient completeness. GI sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. Ontology codes are defensible exact matches. All values are internally consistent.
notes: Dietary fibre is confirmed zero for whole cow's milk — naturally zero as milk contains no plant cell-wall material. Iron is confirmed zero per product label and USDA SR Legacy for whole milk. Vitamin C is confirmed zero per product label; UHT processing destroys residual ascorbic acid. Per-100g values derived from product NIP reported per 100ml, adjusted using whole milk density of approximately 1.03 g/ml; this introduces a minor rounding effect of less than 2% on all values. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values are not reported on the Meiji Singapore NIP and are set to -1 (not reported). Water content not reported on NIP; set to -1. The food_subgroup field in the input was truncated; full description restored in this output. Region tags in the input contained narrative text rather than structured region codes; corrected in this output.
